commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces hardcoded reusable-workflow versions with a release placeholder, adds Dependabot guidance, and removes duplicate workflow setup text from the package README.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR changes package-publishing documentation rather than reporting a runtime defect.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer must approve the permanent downstream trust model; the mechanical revision is narrow, but the collaborator-authored PR and security-policy choice require human handling.

target.title: docs: recommend pinned package publish workflow
target.body:
## Summary

- update the package publish workflow example to use a `<CURRENT_CLAWHUB_RELEASE>` placeholder instead of a hardcoded workflow version
- add Dependabot `github-actions` setup guidance so publishers get update PRs for newer official workflow releases
- remove the GitHub Actions workflow section from the npm package README so the package README does not carry stale workflow setup docs

## Tests

- `git diff --check origin/main -- docs/cli.md packages/clawhub/README.md`
- `bun run format:check -- docs/cli.md packages/clawhub/README.md`
target.author: patrick-erichsen

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: Fix stale cron catch-up after schedule updates
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91944.

Cron restart catch-up can infer a missed cron slot from `lastRunAtMs` even when `nextRunAtMs` already points to the future. That catch-up path is valid for ordinary restart recovery, but after `cron.update` changes scheduling inputs it can replay an old slot that predates the new schedule's activation.

This change records when schedule/enabled inputs are updated and makes startup catch-up skip inferred cron slots older than that activation time. The focused regression covers the reported monthly schedule shape, an isolated `agentTurn`, and the 120s startup deferral path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a cron job updated from a day-10 monthly schedule to a day-11 monthly schedule no longer gets treated as a missed day-11 slot from the previous month and deferred to `gateway startup + 120s` on restart.

Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout at `26b5b5efb9` with Node 24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts --testNamePattern "does not defer a cron slot that predates a schedule update|replays the most recent missed cron slot|defers overdue isolated"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service.issue-regressions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

Evidence after fix: the new `#91944` regression updates the monthly cron expression through the cron service, restarts at the reported next-day window with `startupDeferredMissedAgentJobDelayMs: 120000`, and asserts that no isolated agent run, system event, or heartbeat is requested.

Observed result after fix: `nextRunAtMs` remains at the real future day-11 run, `lastRunAtMs` remains the previous actual day-10 run, and the restart catch-up tests pass. The full focused verification above also passed, including local `check:changed`.

What was not tested: no live gateway upgrade, launchd double-restart, or Telegram delivery was run; the proof is a source-level service regression for the scheduler state sequence from the issue.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts` - 15 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts` - 25 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service.issue-regressions.test.ts` - 43 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts` - 66 passed
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

Remote `crabbox-wrapper` changed-check attempts were not usable in this shell: default Azure lacked CLI/auth, Blacksmith Testbox requires Crabbox >= 0.22.0 while the local binary is 0.20.0, and local-container lacked Docker. The same changed check completed locally after dependencies were installed.
target.author: turbotheturtle
